title: Smoke-Hot Xerid Ukrainian Buckwheat Pilaf
description: A savory, crunchy, and filling Ukrainian dish made with buckwheat, smoked paprika, and a blend of spices, suitable for Buddhist temple-friendly meals and hangover cures.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Mince the garlic and add it to the saucepan, cooking for an additional 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and dried thyme, stirring to combine.
Add the buckwheat groats and water to the saucepan, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover.
Simmer for 20 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the buckwheat is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
Optional: add chopped fresh herbs, such as parsley or dill, for garnish.
